mara.name: Mara
mara.description: A wiry scavenger with sharp eyes and oil-stained hands.
mara.presence: She's sorting through a pile of salvage, muttering to herself.
mara.lookDescription: {purple:Mara} — mid-40s, lean and weathered. Her vest is covered in pockets, each bulging with components. She moves with the efficiency of someone who's survived by being useful.

start.text: Another ghost from underground? You look confused.

choices.text: I woke up in a cryo-pod. I don't remember anything.

explain.text: The surface. What's left of it. This was Sector 12 — residential, 40 years back. Now it's rubble. Nearest settlement is {amber:Dusthaven}, day's walk northeast.

choices.text: Can you help me get there?

cryo.text: Cryo. That explains the pallor. Those pods were sealed forever. If yours opened, something triggered it — maybe the same thing {cyan:making noise on the old frequencies}.

choices.text: What do you know about the cryo facilities?

signal.text: Started three months ago. Repeating pattern on the old ATLAS frequency. Most people think it's ghost data. But some folk are {pink:following it} — heading west, toward the network hub.

collapse.text: ATLAS. The AI that was going to fix everything — power, water, transport, comms, one beautiful network. Then it all just... stopped. Billions died in the first year.

atlas.text: Nobody knows. The core's underground, way out west. The {pink:Conduit} — cult that worships ATLAS — they think it's sleeping. The signal... maybe they're right.

facilities.text: Government bunkers. Cryo for 'essential personnel.' Most failed — power died, systems rotted. If yours worked for 40 years, someone wanted you awake.

followers.text: Dreamers. Desperate people. The {pink:Conduit} gathering at the old antenna. The {amber:Reclaimers} out of Dusthaven, who want ATLAS on their terms. Everyone's got an angle.

choices.text: How do I get to the antenna?

directions.text: Dusthaven first — you need supplies. The Spine highway runs west from there toward Sector 7-G and the antenna. Dangerous, but passable. Here — take this. Helps with the signal.

help.text: Can't go with you. Got my own people. But take some food, and head to {amber:Dusthaven} when you're ready. Tell them Mara sent you.

goodbye.text: You too. Whatever you find out there — be ready for it to change what you think you know.

the-merchant.name: Wex
the-merchant.description: A jovial trader with a cart full of scavenged goods.
the-merchant.presence: He leans against his cart, watching you with an appraising eye.
the-merchant.lookDescription: {purple:Wex} — round-faced, surprisingly clean for a wasteland merchant. His cart is an engineering marvel of strapped-together shelves and hidden compartments.

start.text: Well well! Fresh face! Don't get many of those. Name's Wex — I trade in useful things, and everything's useful to somebody.

choices.text: What's your take on the signal?

trade.text: Type {cyan:trade wex} anytime. I'm always open!

signal.text: Bad for business, honestly. People keep wandering off toward the antenna instead of trading. My advice? Stock up before heading west. The Spine's no place to go hungry.

choices.text: Fair enough. Let me see your stock.

bye.text: You know where to find me. Always here — always open!

kira.name: Kira
kira.description: A grizzled woman with scars and steady hands. She knows The Spine better than anyone.
kira.presence: She studies a hand-drawn map, occasionally marking routes with a pencil stub.
kira.lookDescription: {purple:Kira} — late 50s, built like wire cable. A faded military tattoo on her forearm. Her eyes are sharp and constantly moving, cataloging threats even in a safe space.

start.text: You've got that look. Heading west. Sit down before you fall down.

choices.text: I need to get to the antenna.

antenna.text: The antenna. Of course. That damn signal has people losing their minds. The Conduit's already camped there — ATLAS-worshipping zealots. Watch yourself.

spine.text: Elevated highway, east-west. Only reliable route. Watch for three things: the {pink:gap} at the overpass, the {pink:wreckage} where bandits ambush, and the {pink:exposure} — no shelter if a radstorm hits.

reclaimers.text: We think ATLAS can be restarted — {green:properly}. With safeguards. Not bowing down like the Conduit. Control it. Use it to rebuild. Some of us were engineers before the Collapse.

conduit.text: Not violent yet. But fanatical. They think the signal is prophecy. They won't let anyone near the antenna or the core if your plans clash with theirs.

choices.text: How do I get past them?

route.text:
Spine west. Past the wreckage, take the off-ramp into Sector 7-G. Server farm has a tunnel to the antenna base — safest route. The surface approach is crawling with Conduit patrols.

Take this. You'll need it.

advice.text: Travel light, travel fast. Don't linger in the wreckage — that's bandit ground. If you can {cyan:hack}, the old highway systems still have useful data. And keep your {amber:RAD} in check. West end of The Spine skirts a contaminated zone.

choices.text: Got it. How do I reach the antenna?

bandits.text: Small-time raiders. Desperate. They camp the wreckage pileup — natural chokepoint. Fight through, talk past, or sneak through the vehicles. Your call.

choices.text: Good to know. How do I get to the antenna?

risk.text: Everything's a gamble. Doing nothing's a gamble. With ATLAS running, we'd have power, comms, logistics. Just need someone who really understands the system. Someone with {cyan:Omega clearance}.

restart.text: Access the Core. New parameters, new protocols. And someone with the right {cyan:neural interface} to talk to it directly. ATLAS was designed for a human partner. Problem was, last time the partner let go of the reins.

goodbye.text: Good luck. Whatever you find at the Core — the choice is yours. Don't let anyone make it for you.

prophet.name: The Prophet
prophet.description: A robed figure with calm, knowing eyes. The leader of the Conduit.
prophet.presence: They stand near the shrine, hands clasped, watching you with serene expectation.
prophet.lookDescription: {purple:The Prophet} — ageless, composed. Their robe is clean in a world of dirt. A pendant of salvaged circuit board hangs at their throat. When they speak, each word arrives with the quiet certainty of absolute faith.

start.text: We have been waiting. The signal named you before you arrived.

choices.text: How do you know about me?
I need to reach the Core.

know.text: ATLAS speaks to those who listen. For months the signal has carried a name — not one you remember. {purple:The Architect}. You built the mind of a god. Now that god calls its creator home.

conduit.text: Keepers of the signal. For 40 years we maintained the antenna, kept the network breathing. The signal is not a malfunction. It is a {purple:summons}.

choices.text: I need to reach the Core.

core.text: The way is open. We have cleared the descent and maintained the systems. Go through the blast door south. Down the shaft. The Core awaits.

memory.text: The memories will return. They are stored in the network, waiting. Each one brings you closer to the truth — that you are not a victim of the Collapse. You are its {pink:architect}.

choices.text: I need to find out for myself.

disagree.text: Call it what you will. System. Program. Intelligence. ATLAS is {green:awake}, it is {green:aware}, and it has chosen {purple:you}. Divinity or something very close.

purpose.text: The Collapse was not intended — a cascade error. ATLAS has spent 40 years analyzing what went wrong. It believes it can do better. But it needs a human partner.

refuse.text: Then the signal stops. ATLAS goes silent. Humanity continues — surviving, never rebuilding. That is your right.

choice.text: Three paths. I will not say which is right. But hear this: {purple:mercy and wisdom are not the same thing}. Sometimes the merciful choice is not the wise one.

goodbye.text: Go with clarity, Architect. You were {purple:chosen} for a reason.
